Welcome to the course six scenario, a salary negotiation. We're going to be putting all of the skills you've learned so far to the test. Alright, here's the situation. After nailing the interview with ABC Corporation, they call you in to offer you the position. Let's say that your desired salary is $60,000. Your goal is to bend the boss's reality and get him to agree to your salary while feeling like he's made a wonderful decision for the company. After shaking the boss's hand and taking a seat, he says, congratulations. We'd like to offer you the position that you've applied for. We were very impressed with your interview and resume and we would like to welcome you to the team. But before we can finalize the paperwork, we just have to settle on the financial details. The typical starting salary for this position is $40,000. Alright, how do you respond?
